author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
verses:
gurau mantre nāmni prabhuvara⋅śacīgarbhaja⋅pade
svarūpe śrī⋅rūpe gaṇayuji tadīyaprathamaje
girīndre gāndharvāsarasi madhurpuyyāṁ vrajavane
vraje bhakte goṣṭhālayiṣu paramāstāṁ mama ratiḥ

na cānyatra kṣetre haritanusanāthehapi sujanād
rasāsvādaṁ premnā dadhadapi vasāmi kṣaṇamapi
samaṁ tvetadgrāmyāvalibhirabhi tanvannapi kathāṁ
vidhāsye saṁvāsaṁ vrajabhuvana eva pratibhavam

sadā rādhā⋅kṛṣṇocchaladatula⋅khelāsthalayujaṁ
vrajaṁ santyajyaitad⋅yugavirahito‛pi kraṭimapi
punardvārāvatyāṁ jadupatimapi prauḍhavibhavaiḥ
sphurantaṁ tadvācāpi hi nahi calāmīkṣitumapi

gatonmādai rādhā sphurati hariṇā śliṣṭahṛdayā
sphuṭaṁ dvārāvatyāmiti jadi śṛṇomi śrutitaṭe
tadāhaṁ tatraivoddhatamati patāmi vrajapurāt
samuḍḍīya svāntādhikagati⋅khagendrādapi javāt

anādiḥ sādirvā paṭuratimṛdurvā pratipada⋅
pramīlatkāruṇyaḥ praguṇakaruṇāhīna iti vā
mahā-vaikuṇṭheśādhika iha naro vā vrajapate⋅
rayaṁ sūnurgoṣṭhe prati⋅jani mamāstāṁ prabhuvaraḥ

anādṛtyodagītāmapi munigaṇairvaiṇikamukhaiḥ
pravīṇāṁ gāndharvāmapi ca nigamaistat priyatamām
ja ekaṁ govindaṁ bhajati kapaṭī dāmbhikatayā
tadabhyarṇe śīrṇe kṣaṇamapi na jāmi vratamidam

ajāṇḍe rādheti⋅sphuradabhidhayāsiktajanayā⋅
‛nayā sākaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ bhajati ja iha premanamitaḥ
paraṁ prakṣālyetaccaraṇa⋅kamale tajjalamaho
mudā pītvā śaśvacchirasi ca vahāmi pratidinam

parityaktaḥpreyojanasamudayairvāḍhamasudhī⋅
rdurandho nīrandhraṁ kadanabharavārdhau nipatitaḥ
tṛṇaṁ dantairdaṣṭvā caṭubhirabhiyācehadya kṛpayā
svayaṁ śrī⋅gāndharvā svapadanalināntaṁ nayatu mām

vrajotpanna⋅kṣīrāśana⋅vasanapātrādibhirahaṁ
padārthairnirvāhya vyavahṛtimadambhaṁ saniyamaḥ
vasāmīśākuṇḍe giri-kula-vare caiva samaye
mariṣye tu preṣṭhe sarasi jīvādipurataḥ

sphurallakṣmīlakṣmī ⋅vrajavijayilakṣmībhara⋅lasad⋅
vapuḥ śrī⋅gāndharvāsmaranikara⋅divyad giribhṛtoḥ
vidhāsye kuñjādau vividha-varivasyāḥ sa-rabhasaṁ
rahaḥ śrī⋅rūpākhyapriyatamajanasyaiva caramaḥ

kṛtaṁ kenāpyetannīja⋅niyamaśaṁsi⋅stavamimaṁ
paṭhet jo visravdhaḥ priya⋅yugala⋅rūpe‛rpita⋅manāḥ
dṛḍhaṁ goṣṭhe hṛṣṭo vasati⋅vasatiṁ prāpya samaye
mudā rādhā⋅kṛṣṇau bhajati sa hi tenaiva sahitaḥ
en_translation:
Let my supreme love be for the Spiritual Master, the mantra, the Holy Name, and the lotus feet of Lord Caitanya. Let it be for Svarūpa Dāmodara, Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī and his associates, and his elder brother Sanātana Gosvāmī. Let it be for Govardhana Hill, Rādhā-kuṇḍa, Mathurā, and the forest of Vraja. Let it be for the devotees and for all the residents of Vraja.

I will not reside for even a moment in any other place, even if it is a holy place of Lord Hari, or even if I am tasting divine mellows with love from saintly persons there. Rather, I will always make my residence, in every birth, only in the land of Vraja, associating with its inhabitants.

Abandoning Vraja, where the peerless pastimes of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa always overflow, I will not go to see even for a moment the Lord of the Yadus resplendent with great opulence in Dvārakā, even if invited.

If I hear it rumored that Rādhā is appearing in Dvārakā with Lord Hari, then with an agitated mind, I will immediately fly there from Vraja, even faster than Garuḍa.

Whether He is beginningless or has a beginning, whether He is expert or very gentle, whether His compassion blossoms at every step or is seemingly absent, whether He is greater than the Lord of Vaikuṇṭha or just a human being here—let this Son of Nanda Mahārāja in Vraja be my only Lord in every birth.

This is my vow: I will not go for even a moment near that deceitful and arrogant person who worships Lord Govinda alone, disregarding Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī, who is most dear to the Vedas and is sung by all the sages.

Whoever worships Lord Kṛṣṇa along with Rādhā, bowing down with love—I will wash his lotus feet, drink that water with joy, and always carry it on my head every day.

Utterly bewildered, having abandoned all beneficial purposes, and deeply fallen into an ocean of misery, I bite a blade of grass between my teeth and humbly beg today: "Let Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī herself lead me to Her lotus feet!"

Sustaining my life without pride with the milk, food, and clothes produced in Vraja, I will reside at Rādhā-kuṇḍa and Govardhana Hill. And I will die at that most beloved lake in front of the devotees.

As the ultimate follower of the most beloved Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, I will eagerly and secretly perform various services in the groves for Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī and for Lord Kṛṣṇa, who is resplendent with the beauty of many Cupids.

He who reads with faith this hymn, which proclaims his own vow, with his mind offered to the Divine Couple, will certainly attain a firm and joyful residence in Vraja and will worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa along with Their associates.
